summary: Closed, unmerged PR that proposed a code-shape improvement for `lb_8001B14C` in `lbcardnew.c`: remove a cached `lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry* snapshot_entries` local and write directly through `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries`. The stated goal was closer target matching; decomp.dev reported `main/melee/lb/lbcardnew` `lb_8001B14C` improving from 63.23% to 98.77%, with the remaining diff described as an `r5`/`r6` allocation swap in a compiler-unrolled linked-list copy loop.

key_files.role: Only changed file; adjusted `lb_8001B14C` snapshot-entry writes.

what_changed: Removed the local declaration `lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry* snapshot_entries;` from `lb_8001B14C`.
Changed linked-list copy-loop writes from `snapshot_entries[j].time/file_no/blocks` to direct `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries[j].time/file_no/blocks` writes.
Changed the cleanup/fill loop from `snapshot_entries[i].file_no = -1` to `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries[i].file_no = -1`.
No behavioral intent is described beyond improving match/code shape; the PR body frames this as a closer representation of the target code.
smart_moves: Targeted a very small source-level change that affects code generation without restructuring the function logic.
Avoided hiding a global struct member behind a local pointer alias when the target apparently keeps direct `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries` accesses.
Preserved existing field names and loop structure while changing only the addressing form used for snapshot entry writes.
Documented verification with `python configure.py && ninja` and gave exact matching status for `main/melee/lb/lbcardnew`.
Called out the remaining blocker explicitly, narrowing future work to the reported `r5`/`r6` allocation swap in the unrolled linked-list copy loop.
decomp_lessons: For matching, a seemingly cleaner local pointer alias can worsen codegen; direct global/member accesses may better preserve the target register allocation and memory-addressing pattern.
When a function is mostly logic-correct but mismatching, try changing variable lifetime and aliasing before making larger structural edits.
Keep small matching PRs evidence-driven: this slice ties the change directly to a decomp.dev improvement from 63.23% to 98.77% for `lb_8001B14C`.
When one stubborn register-allocation issue remains, record the exact symptom so later work can search for and continue from it instead of rediscovering it.
naming_conventions: `lb_8001B14C` remained an address-based/generated function name in this PR.
`lb_80432A68` remained an address-based global with known member `snapshot_entries` and still-unknown member `unk_34`.
Snapshot entry typing used the module-prefixed type name `lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry`.
The local linked-list helper type was named `struct SnapshotNode`, with fields evidenced in the diff/body including `time`, `file_no`, `blocks`, and `next`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Remove a local pointer alias to reduce/register-shift lifetime effects and force direct global member references.
Use direct `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries[index]` writes when the target appears not to materialize a separate snapshot-entry base pointer.
Watch for register allocation swaps such as `r5`/`r6` in compiler-unrolled linked-list copy loops; the PR says this was the remaining diff.
Use decomp.dev per-unit reports to quantify partial-match progress even when a function remains under 100%.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported one unmatched-item improvement: `main/melee/lb/lbcardnew` `lb_8001B14C`, +435 bytes, 63.23% before to 98.77% after.
The PR was closed with `merged_at` absent/null in the context; the slice does not state a closure reason.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `lb_8001B14C` to see whether the remaining `r5`/`r6` allocation swap was solved.
Search for `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries` direct-access patterns in merged code.
Search for other `lbcardnew` snapshot listing changes after PR #2496.
Search decomp.dev reports for `main/melee/lb/lbcardnew` moving from 98.77% to 100%.
